steps.title: Replace the repeated ratio
steps.content: The second equation contains $\frac{x}{y}$. Rewrite it as $$\frac{30x}{ny}=\frac{30}{n}\cdot\frac{x}{y}=5$$

steps.title: Substitute the known value
steps.content: Since $\frac{x}{y}=5$, substitute $5$: $$\frac{30}{n}\cdot 5=5$$ Divide both sides by $5$ to get $$\frac{30}{n}=1$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content: $\frac{30}{n}=1$ means the numerator and denominator must be equal, so $n=30$. This also makes the original second expression $\frac{30x}{30y}=\frac{x}{y}=5$, which checks. Enter 30.
